security_gap.gap_description: Vote input may be spoofed without signature verification.

actor_risk.description: The actor is unauthenticated and can perform governance actions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: The system accepts unsigned votes from external actors.

assumptions: The governance contract does not enforce signature verification for vote messages.
The actor is able to access the voting interface without authentication.

mitigation.strategy: Require signature-based vote authentication and role validation.

description: An attacker may impersonate a user and submit fraudulent votes to the governance contract.

security_gap.gap_description: Malicious actors can alter the vote data before it reaches the contract.

actor_risk.description: The actor is unauthenticated and can tamper with the voting process.

behavioral_risk.rationale: The system lacks data integrity checks during vote submission.

assumptions: The system does not validate data integrity of vote messages.
The actor has control of the communication channel between the user interface and the contract.

mitigation.strategy: Implement data integrity checks using cryptographic hashing or digital signatures.

description: An attacker may alter the contents of a vote before it is processed by the contract, potentially changing the outcome of the governance decision.

security_gap.gap_description: Lack of audit trail to track votes and verify users.

actor_risk.description: Unauthenticated users can submit votes without leaving an audit trail.

behavioral_risk.rationale: The absence of non-repudiation mechanisms allows users to deny their votes.

assumptions: The system does not maintain a verifiable audit trail of votes.
The system does not implement any mechanism for binding votes to the user identity.

mitigation.strategy: Implement a secure audit trail using blockchain technology or a trusted third-party logging service.

description: An attacker may cast votes and then deny having done so, making it difficult to hold them accountable.

security_gap.gap_description: Vote information may be exposed before it is recorded on the blockchain.

actor_risk.description: Malicious actors may attempt to gain access to sensitive user voting data.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Vote data is transmitted across the network and may be intercepted.

assumptions: The system transmits vote data in an insecure manner (e.g., over HTTP).
The actor has access to network traffic monitoring capabilities.

mitigation.strategy: Use HTTPS or other secure protocols for all communication channels related to vote submission.

description: An attacker may intercept or eavesdrop on vote data before it is processed by the governance contract, gaining knowledge of user votes.

security_gap.gap_description: The system may become unavailable due to a large volume of malicious vote requests.

actor_risk.description: Malicious actors can flood the voting interface with false requests.

behavioral_risk.rationale: The system does not implement rate limiting on vote submissions.

assumptions: The system lacks effective rate limiting or input validation for vote requests.
The actor has sufficient resources to generate a high volume of malicious requests.

mitigation.strategy: Implement rate limiting and input validation measures to mitigate denial-of-service attacks.

description: An attacker may flood the system with vote requests to make it unavailable for legitimate users.
